title: Find the starting point in LL

content.problemStatement: Given the head of a linked list that may contain a cycle, return the starting point of that cycle. If there is no cycle in the linked list return null.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the linked list <= 10^4
The linked list may or may not contain a cycle
content.inputFormat: The input is the head of a linked list where each node contains an integer value.
content.outputFormat: The output is the node where the cycle starts. If there is no cycle, the output is null.

sampleTestCases.output:
3
**Explanation: **This linked list contains a loop of size 3 starting at node with value 3.

NULL
**Explanation: **This linked list does not contain a loop hence has no starting point

solution.approach: Use a hash set to store visited nodes. Traverse the linked list and check if a node is already in the set. If it is, return that node as the start of the cycle. If not, add the node to the set and continue traversing.
